steps.title: Find base angles of isosceles triangle ACD
steps.content:
Since $AC = CD$ (given) and $\angle ACD = 108°$ (given), triangle $ACD$ is isosceles. The base angles opposite the equal sides are equal:
$$\angle CAD = \angle CDA = \frac{180° - 108°}{2} = \frac{72°}{2} = 36°$$

steps.title: Use the crossing angle at D
steps.content:
At $D$, $\angle BDE$ is the same angle as $\angle CDA$, so
$$\angle BDE=36°.$$

steps.title: Find the angle at E inside triangle BDE
steps.content:
Since $B$, $C$, and $D$ are collinear in the figure, $\angle DBE=\angle EBC=29°$. In triangle $BDE$:
$$\angle DEB=180°-29°-36°=115°.$$

steps.title: Use the straight angle at E
steps.content:
$\angle DEB$ and $x°$ form a straight line, so they sum to $180°$:
$$x=180°-115°=65°$$
The answer is 65.
